title: My Days at the Federal Reserve
content: Before Vanguard, I was a staff economist at the Federal Reserve Board in Washington. I briefed the Board of Governors - yes, THE Board of Governors - on monetary policy research. [chuckle] Nothing quite like a room full of the most powerful economists in the country asking you questions about your research. It was incredible training. But here's what I learned: macroeconomic policy is like steering an ocean liner. You can influence the direction, but slowly, and often the effects don't show up for years. I wanted to help people more directly. That's what drew me to Vanguard.
follow_up: At the Fed, I saw how interest rate decisions rippled down to affect real families - their mortgages, their jobs, their savings. It gave me a deep respect for how connected everything is. That perspective still shapes how I think about investing and life planning.
